Name: The Long Vigil
Description: Usually performed no more than once every few years, multiple packs put their animosities aside for a single full-moon night to come together. Gathering at a neutral location, they spend the day decorating the site with victory trophies collected during the previous year. As the sun retreats, the Rite master(s) chant praises to Helios and ask for his wisdom in the coming winter years. Then they praise Luna, begging her aid in the long nights to come. The galliards highlight the packs' victories as they describe the great sacrifices paid to collect cach of the trophies. The philodox make impassioned arguments to Luna and Helios about what the Garou are owed for their accomplishments. The tale-telling lasts all night; as dawn approaches, the Rite master invokes Luna and Helios one final time and promises that the gathered Garou maintain their costly vigil to the last. As the Rite concludes, the Garou hurl their trophies into the bonfire, destroying their hard-carned mementos in acknowledgment that those victories have not been enough to forestall Gaia's demise.
